name: Monte Areo Archaeological Loop
name_original: Mirador de la Peña' l Carru a Cimavilla — circular
description: This difficult 9.5-mile (15.3 km) jogging route offers panoramic coastal views and historical sites near Cimavilla, Gijón.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Carreño offer diverse terrain, from coastal paths to inland trails. The region is characterized by its access to significant coastal attractions, including beaches and scenic overlooks. Runners can explore a network of trails that range from dedicated running paths to routes shared with hiking and cycling. This variety provides options for different experiences, from flat seaside jogs to more undulating paths through natural features. Best jogging routes around Carreño The most popular jogging route is Perlora by the Vía Verde del Tranqueru to Playa Xibares, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, offering a moderate experience along a greenway. Another top favourite among local runners is Candás - Luanco Coastal Trail, a moderate 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This route provides scenic views along the coastline, connecting two charming towns. Local runners also love the Monte Areo Archaeological Loop, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail leading through an archaeological landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Carreño is defined by coastal paths, beaches, and varied inland trails, including archaeological sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy jogs to more challenging runs. The routes in Carreño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Carreño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Carreño?
A: Carreño offers a diverse selection of over 15 running routes, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include coastal paths, greenways, and trails through archaeological landscapes, ensuring a varied experience for every runner.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging trails in Carreño?
A: Yes, Carreño has several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. Approximately a quarter of the routes are classified as easy, providing gentle terrain and enjoyable scenery. For example, the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo beach&lt;/a&gt; offers flat, accessible options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Carreño's jogging routes?
A: The jogging routes in Carreño feature a mix of terrain. You'll find coastal paths with stunning sea views, sections along greenways like the Vía Verde del Tranqueru, and more undulating trails through natural and archaeological areas. This variety allows runners to choose between paved surfaces, compacted dirt paths, and more rugged trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Carreño?
A: Many of Carreño's routes are suitable for families, especially those classified as easy or moderate. These trails often pass through scenic areas or along beaches, providing enjoyable outings for all ages. Look for routes that avoid significant elevation changes for a more comfortable family experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Carreño?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the outdoor trails in Carreño, especially those away from urban centers. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash and check local regulations, particularly around protected natural areas or beaches during peak seasons. Ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there circular jogging routes available in Carreño?
A: Yes, Carreño features several excellent circular jogging routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056090912/monte-areo-archaeological-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Areo Archaeological Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a 9.5-mile (15.3 km) run through an intriguing historical landscape. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15001664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candás – Perlora Holiday Town loop from Xivares&lt;/a&gt;, providing a coastal circuit.

Q: What scenic highlights or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Carreño's running routes offer numerous scenic highlights. You can enjoy coastal views along trails connecting towns like Candás and Luanco, or pass by significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4016988" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo Peñas Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which sits on a dramatic cliff. Other routes might lead you past historical sites like those found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056090912/monte-areo-archaeological-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Areo Archaeological Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Carreño?
A: Carreño is enjoyable for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, ideal for longer runs. Summer is great for coastal jogs, especially in the mornings or evenings to avoid the midday heat. Even winter can be suitable, particularly on milder days, with the added benefit of fewer crowds.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the sea or beaches?
A: Absolutely! Carreño is known for its stunning coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056081441/candas-luanco-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candás - Luanco Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering continuous scenic views of the Cantabrian Sea. Many routes also provide access to beautiful beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo beach&lt;/a&gt; and Playa Xibares, perfect for a seaside run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Carreño?
A: The komoot community highly rates the running experience in Carreño, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the region's diverse landscapes, from invigorating coastal paths with breathtaking views to tranquil inland trails. The variety of routes, catering to different abilities, is also a commonly highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any routes that combine natural beauty with historical or cultural sites?
A: Yes, Carreño offers unique routes that blend natural beauty with historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056090912/monte-areo-archaeological-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Areo Archaeological Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, allowing you to run through an ancient landscape while enjoying the natural surroundings. You might also encounter historical monuments or charming towns along coastal trails.

Q: How long are the typical running trails in Carreño?
A: The running trails in Carreño vary in length, offering options for different preferences. You can find shorter routes, such as the 3.5-mile (5.6 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056081441/candas-luanco-coastal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candás - Luanco Coastal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, or longer runs like the 9.5-mile (15.3 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056090912/monte-areo-archaeological-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Areo Archaeological Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The average distance for a moderate route is around 6 miles (10 km).
tips: Legend has it that some 'xanas' lived in this fountain who took care of the laundry of the town's neighbors when they left it lying out in the night watch. One of the stones has the name "Herminia" engraved on it.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Mirador de la Peña' l Carru a Cimavilla circular jogging route?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as difficult. It requires a good level of fitness and sure-footedness due to its varied terrain, which includes winding and steep paths, as well as quartzite crests.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, featuring a mix of road, hiking paths, tracks, and streets. You'll encounter unpaved sections, asphalt, natural paths, paved areas, and some 'alpine' sections with potentially rocky or uneven ground, including quartzite crests.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the expansive views from the Peña'l Carru Viewpoint, you can also discover the Les Xanes Spring and the historic Santa Eulalia Church. The area also boasts historical elements like the Camino de la Caleya La Cabiana and archaeological remains such as dolmens near Monte Areo.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador de la Peña' l Carru a Cimavilla circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife or other trail users. Be prepared for the challenging sections if bringing a pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant conditions for jogging, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, and winter might present challenges with colder weather or potentially slippery conditions on some sections.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Mirador de la Peña' l Carru a Cimavilla circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically look for parking near Cimavilla or the general vicinity of the Mirador de la Peña'l Carru. Public transport options to Gijón and then local connections might also be available, but direct trailhead access via public transport can vary.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Mirador de la Peña'l Carru?
faqs.answer: From the Peña'l Carru Viewpoint, you can expect expansive panoramic views of the coastal sector of the Cabo Peñas region, stretching from the Ría de Avilés to the Ría de Aboño. You'll also get views of Monte Areo.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other paths, including significant portions of the Ruta Xixon - Puertu Payares and the Northern Route (Asturia), as well as smaller segments of the Camin de les Fazones and the Camín Real.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this challenging trail?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, it's advisable to bring appropriate jogging shoes with good grip, sufficient water, snacks, a map or GPS device, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A first-aid kit is also recommended.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is natural, the nearby areas of Cimavilla and Gijón offer various cafes, restaurants, and other facilities where you can refuel before or after your jog.
